<objective>
Scaffold the Payload CMS + Next.js project from the website template, strip template-specific content, upgrade to Next.js 16.2.0-canary, and establish the core Payload configuration with blocksAsJSON storage, block registry pattern, and Lexical editor.

Purpose: Creates the foundational project that all subsequent phases build on. The blocksAsJSON and blockReferences patterns MUST be established before any blocks are created.
Output: A buildable Payload CMS project with PostgreSQL JSONB block storage, empty block registry, and RSC-ready App Router structure.
</objective>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Scaffold from website template, strip template content, upgrade Next.js</name>
  <files>package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, next.config.ts, tsconfig.json, .env, src/app/*, src/blocks/*, src/heros/*, src/Header/*, src/Footer/*, src/components/*</files>
  <action>
1. Run `pnpx create-payload-app@latest -t website` into the current directory (the project root is currently empty). Use `--db postgres` if the CLI supports it, otherwise select PostgreSQL when prompted. Set the project name to "payload-starter".

2. After scaffold completes, upgrade Next.js to 16.2.0-canary per user decision:
   ```
   pnpm add next@16.2.0-canary.95
   ```
   Note: Using canary per user decision despite stability concern. Payload 3.79+ supports Next.js 16.2.0+.

3. Strip template-specific content (the user locked "remove all 5 existing blocks, frontend pages, dark mode, hero fields"):
   - DELETE the entire `src/heros/` directory (hero is a section block, not separate fields)
   - DELETE all block directories inside `src/blocks/` (Hero, Content, Media, CTA, Archive blocks -- the exact names may vary, delete ALL existing block folders)
   - DELETE the frontend page rendering files that import these blocks (check `src/app/(frontend)/[slug]/page.tsx`, `src/app/(frontend)/posts/` etc.)
   - DELETE dark mode toggle component(s) -- look for ThemeSelector, ThemeProvider, or similar
   - DELETE `src/Header/` and `src/Footer/` directories (will be rebuilt in Phase 3)
   - Keep the `(payload)` route group intact (admin panel routes)
   - Keep the `(frontend)` route group structure but simplify to a minimal layout.tsx and page.tsx

4. Keep these utilities (user locked "keep useful utilities"):
   - `src/utilities/generateMeta.ts`
   - `src/utilities/mergeOpenGraph.ts`
   - `src/utilities/getServerSideURL.ts`
   - Any other utility files that don't depend on the stripped blocks

5. Clean up imports -- after stripping, fix any broken imports in remaining files. The frontend layout.tsx should be a minimal RSC layout. The frontend page.tsx should render a simple "Payload Starter" heading.

6. Update `next.config.ts` to ensure it uses `withPayload` wrapper and has `output: 'standalone'` for Docker/VPS deployment:
   ```typescript
   import { withPayload } from '@payloadcms/next/withPayload'
   const nextConfig = {
     output: 'standalone',
   }
   export default withPayload(nextConfig)
   ```

7. Ensure `.env` has `DATABASE_URL` pointing to a local PostgreSQL database (e.g., `postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/payload_starter`). If the template created `.env.example`, copy it to `.env` and fill in the database URL.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd C:/Projects/sandbox/payload-starter && ls package.json src/app 2>&1</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>Project scaffolded with template blocks/hero/dark mode removed. Next.js 16.2.0-canary installed. Utilities preserved. Clean (frontend) and (payload) route groups. package.json and src/app/ exist.</done>
</task>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 2: Configure Payload core -- blocksAsJSON, block registry, Lexical, project structure</name>
  <files>src/payload.config.ts, src/blocks/registry.ts, src/blocks/RenderBlocks.tsx, src/fields/styleOptions.ts, src/collections/Users.ts, src/collections/Media.ts</files>
  <action>
1. Create the block registry at `src/blocks/registry.ts`:
   ```typescript
   import type { Block } from 'payload'

   // Phase 1: Empty registry, pattern established
   // Phase 2 will populate with atomic blocks
   export const allBlocks: Block[] = []

   // Slug arrays for blockReferences in collection fields
   export const atomicBlockSlugs: string[] = []
   export const sectionBlockSlugs: string[] = []
   export const allBlockSlugs: string[] = [...atomicBlockSlugs, ...sectionBlockSlugs]
   ```

2. Create the RenderBlocks mapper at `src/blocks/RenderBlocks.tsx`:
   ```typescript
   import type { ReactNode } from 'react'

   // Block component registry -- populated in Phase 2
   const blockComponents: Record<string, React.ComponentType<any>> = {}

   type Props = {
     blocks: Array<{ blockType: string; id?: string; [key: string]: unknown }>
   }

   export function RenderBlocks({ blocks }: Props): ReactNode {
     if (!blocks?.length) return null

     return (
       <>
         {blocks.map((block, i) => {
           const Component = blockComponents[block.blockType]
           if (!Component) return null
           return <Component key={block.id ?? i} {...block} />
         })}
       </>
     )
   }
   ```

3. Create the shared style options skeleton at `src/fields/styleOptions.ts`:
   ```typescript
   import type { GroupField } from 'payload'

   // Shared style group field -- added to every block via block registry pattern
   // SKELETON: Phase 2 will add the remaining 7 style fields:
   //   - padding (hybrid preset sm/md/lg/xl + custom)
   //   - margin (hybrid preset sm/md/lg/xl + custom)
   //   - borderRadius (hybrid preset sm/md/lg/xl + custom)
   //   - borderWidth (hybrid preset sm/md/lg/xl + custom)
   //   - textSize (hybrid preset sm/md/lg/xl + custom)
   //   - backgroundColor (theme palette presets + custom color picker)
   //   - textColor (theme palette presets + custom color picker)
   //
   // All 8 properties are per user decision. Only customCSS is implemented here
   // as a placeholder to establish the field group structure.
   export const styleOptions: GroupField = {
     name: 'styles',
     type: 'group',
     label: 'Styles',
     admin: {
       condition: () => true, // Always show, placeholder for future tab integration
     },
     fields: [
       {
         name: 'customCSS',
         type: 'text',
         label: 'Custom CSS Classes',
         admin: {
           description: 'Additional Tailwind classes to apply',
         },
       },
     ],
   }
   ```

4. Update `src/payload.config.ts` to wire everything together:
   - Import `allBlocks` from `@/blocks/registry`
   - Configure `postgresAdapter` with `blocksAsJSON: true` (CRITICAL -- cannot be retrofitted)
   - Configure `lexicalEditor()` as the default editor (no custom config needed for Phase 1)
   - Register `allBlocks` at the top level via `blocks: allBlocks`
   - Keep Users and Media collections (clean up any template-specific fields that reference stripped blocks)
   - Set `typescript.outputFile` to `src/payload-types.ts`
   - Import and use `sharp` for image processing

   The final config should match this structure:
   ```typescript
   import { buildConfig } from 'payload'
   import { postgresAdapter } from '@payloadcms/db-postgres'
   import { lexicalEditor } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'
   import sharp from 'sharp'
   import { allBlocks } from '@/blocks/registry'
   import { Users } from '@/collections/Users'
   import { Media } from '@/collections/Media'

   export default buildConfig({
     admin: {
       user: Users.slug,
     },
     db: postgresAdapter({
       pool: { connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL },
       blocksAsJSON: true,
     }),
     editor: lexicalEditor(),
     blocks: allBlocks,
     collections: [Users, Media],
     globals: [],
     sharp,
     typescript: {
       outputFile: 'src/payload-types.ts',
     },
   })
   ```

5. Clean up `src/collections/Users.ts` -- keep the standard admin user collection. Remove any fields that reference stripped template content.

6. Clean up `src/collections/Media.ts` -- keep the standard media upload collection. Remove any fields that reference stripped template content. Ensure it has sensible image sizes configured.

7. Ensure `src/globals/` directory exists (empty for Phase 1).
  </action>
